items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` **Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/` **Triggered by:** PR #58407 by `@m-redding` ## What Changed PR #58407 updated the `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` README and CHANGELOG, adding entries for AZC0020 and AZC0101 and simplifying the README for this internal-only package. It also added individual documentation files `docs/AZC0020.md` and `docs/AZC0101.md`. ## Gaps Found - `docs/AZC0012.md` does not exist ŌĆö AZC0012 (Avoid single word type names) is the only implemented rule without its own dedicated documentation file. AZC0020 and AZC0101 both have individual `docs/.md` files. - The README rules table lists all three rules (`AZC0012`, `AZC0020`, `AZC0101`) but does not hyperlink any of them to their individual documentation pages. AZC0020 and AZC0101 have standalone docs that should be linked; AZC0012 needs its own doc file created first.
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Create `docs/AZC0012.md` **Path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0012.md` **Action:** Create the file Use `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0020.md` and `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0101.md` as templates. The content should include: ```markdown # AZC0012: Avoid single word type names | Property | Value | |----------|-------| | **Severity** | Warning | | **Code fix** | No | ## Cause Public types (classes, interfaces, or structs) in Azure SDK namespaces (`Azure.*`, excluding `Azure.Core.*`) use single-word names, which are too generic and risk colliding with BCL types or types from other libraries. ## Why this matters Single-word type names such as `Format`, `Manager`, or `Options` are highly likely to conflict with types already defined in the .NET BCL or other libraries, leading to ambiguous references and confusing API surfaces. ## How to fix Use descriptive multi-word names. Add a prefix or suffix that describes the purpose or domain of the type: ```diff namespace Azure.Data.Tables { - public class Format { } + public class TableDataFormat { } } ``` ## When to suppress warnings Suppress only if the type name is genuinely well-known and intentionally mirrors a BCL type for compatibility reasons. Document the justification: ```csharp #pragma warning disable AZC0012 // Justified: intentional alias for well-known BCL type public class Stream { } #pragma warning restore AZC0012 ``` ## Example of a violation ```csharp namespace Azure.Data.Tables { public class Format { } // ŌØī single-word name } ``` ## Example of how to fix ```csharp namespace Azure.Data.Tables { public class TableDataFormat { } // Ō£ģ descriptive multi-word name } ``` ## Notes - Only public types in Azure SDK namespaces (starting with `Azure.` except `Azure.Core.*`) are analyzed. - Interface names starting with `I` have the prefix ignored (e.g., `IClient` is treated as single-word). - Nested types are not analyzed. ``` ### Step 2: Update the README rules table to link to individual docs **Path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md` **Action:** Edit ŌĆö replace the existing `## Implemented Rules` table Current table: ```markdown | Rule | Description | Fix | |------|-------------|-----| | **AZC0012** | Avoid single word type names | ŌĆö | | **AZC0020** | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | ŌĆö | | **AZC0101** | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | Ō£ģ | ``` Replace with: ```markdown | Rule | Description | Fix | |------|-------------|-----| | [**AZC0012**](docs/AZC0012.md) | Avoid single word type names | ŌĆö | | [**AZC0020**](docs/AZC0020.md) | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | ŌĆö | | [**AZC0101**](docs/AZC0101.md) | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | Ō£ģ | ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md` is intentionally simplified for an internal-only analyzer package and does not follow the standard customer-facing template. The current sections are appropriate. No further section changes are required. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding: 1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/` > Note: `Update-Snippets.ps1` and `Export-API.ps1` are not required here ŌĆö no public API surface changed and no snippet placeholders exist in this README.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Tools] Docs: Add missing AZC0012.md and link rules table in README
